spec_match.rationale: Calls `CaptureController.getSupportedZoomLevels()` to populate snap targets and `setZoomLevel(level)` to drive the captured tab's zoom. That is the third leg of the spec exactly as documented.

interactive.rationale: Range slider + button with 6 listeners snap to the supported zoom level and pipe each chosen value through `setZoomLevel`; the current zoom and supported-set readouts update live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `Reads the supported zoom levels, snaps a slider to the discrete set, and shows the captured surface zoom up and down`. Page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Snapping to supported levels is good, but the page doesn't show what happens when the user passes an unsupported level (rejection), when no capture is active, or when the underlying tab refuses (e.g. page-zoom locked by the embedder).

references.rationale: Page has no chromestatus reference link. `see also` is present (listed in sections) but does not cite `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5092615678066688` — invariant #3 violation.

design_system.rationale: 30 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5092615678066688` from `see also`. Invariant #3 — every concept page must cite the chromestatus URL so the slug-recovery script can self-heal the folder. Add the link plus the WICG captured-surface-control spec.

openQuestions.title: Add the origin-trial banner
openQuestions.detail: Feature is OT-only at v136. Per CLAUDE.md concept-policy for OT features, the page must show a visible `to actually run this, join the origin trial` banner at the top.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detect probe
openQuestions.detail: Unlike the other concepts in this folder, the zoom-coach page lacks a probe. Add `typeof CaptureController.prototype.setZoomLevel === 'function'` or similar so users know whether their build exposes the API.

openQuestions.title: Show the unsupported-zoom-level rejection
openQuestions.detail: Pass a value not in `getSupportedZoomLevels()` and surface the resulting rejection so authors know the snap-to-supported-set isn't optional.

summary: Implements the third spec leg (zoom) correctly with a snap slider but ships three CLAUDE.md regressions in one page: missing chromestatus link, no OT banner, and no feature-detect probe. None are content issues — all three are mechanical fixes that the sibling concept pages already have.
